comment: Connector registry. The Node builder reads this to (a) render connector signatures into the generation prompt, (b) copy the right template files into a generated project, and (c) build .env.example from required_env. Keep in sync with the .py files — tool_templates/__init__.py has a check_catalog() that verifies it.

connectors.label: Gmail
connectors.description: Search the user's mail and create draft replies. Creates drafts only — never sends.

tools.name: gmail_search
tools.summary: Search Gmail using Gmail search syntax (e.g. 'from:acme.com is:unread'). Returns sender, subject and snippet per match.

tools.name: gmail_create_draft
tools.signature: gmail_create_draft(to: str, subject: str, body: str, reply_to_message_id: str = '') -> str
tools.summary: Create a draft reply. Does NOT send. Pass reply_to_message_id to thread onto an existing conversation.

connectors.label: Slack
connectors.description: List and read public channels, and post messages. Posting is immediate and cannot be undone.

tools.name: slack_list_channels
tools.summary: List public channels the bot can see, with ids and member counts.

tools.name: slack_read_channel
tools.summary: Read recent messages from a channel by name or id.

tools.name: slack_post_message
tools.summary: Post a message to a channel. Irreversible — use only when the user asked for a message to be sent.

connectors.label: Postgres
connectors.description: Read-only SQL access. Writes and DDL are rejected by both a statement check and a read-only transaction.

tools.name: pg_query
tools.summary: Run ONE read-only SELECT (or WITH ... SELECT) and return rows, capped at 100. Writes, DDL and multi-statement input are rejected.
